parameters.command:
=# knobs
TOP_H=608
GAP=1
PUSH=0
CROP_TOP={{ $json.fields.TopCrop[0] }}
EXTRA_CROP=0

# compute the bottom height
BOTTOM_H=$((1920 - (TOP_H + GAP + PUSH) - EXTRA_CROP))
BOT_Y=$((TOP_H + GAP + PUSH))

ffmpeg -y \
-i "/videos/{{ $('Update Voice').item.json.id }}_xvideo.mp4" \
-i "/videos/{{ $json.id }}_clone.mp4" \
-filter_complex "
color=c=black:size=1080x1920:d=360[bg];
[0:v]scale=1080:${TOP_H}:flags=bicubic[top];
[1:v]scale=1080:-2[vs];
[vs]crop=w=1080:h=${BOTTOM_H}:x=0:y=${CROP_TOP}[bot];
[bg][top]overlay=x=0:y=0[tmp];
[tmp][bot]overlay=x=0:y=${BOT_Y}[v];
[1:a]volume=1.5[a]
" \
-map "[v]" -map "[a]" \
-t "$(ffprobe -v error -show_entries format=duration -of default=noprint_wrappers=1:nokey=1 "/videos/{{ $json.id }}_clone.mp4")" \
-c:v libx264 -pix_fmt yuv420p -crf 18 -preset veryfast \
-c:a aac -movflags +faststart \
/videos/{{ $('Update Voice').item.json.id }}_output_vertical.mp4
=ffmpeg -y -ss 0 -t 3 -i "{{ $('Write Voice Full1').item.json.fileName }}" -acodec copy "/videos/{{ $('(⭐ Create) a record').item.json.id }}3s_voice.mp3"

=#!/bin/sh
set -eu -o pipefail

RID="{{ $('Update Voice').item.json.id }}"
IN_RAW="${RID}_xvideo_9x16.mp4"
FIRST_3="${RID}_output_vertical.mp4"
MUSIC_RAW="${RID}_music.mp3"

OUT="/videos/${RID}_video_only.mp4"

# Resolve absolute paths
case "$IN_RAW"    in /*) IN="$IN_RAW"     ;; *) IN="/videos/$IN_RAW"     ;; esac
case "$FIRST_3"   in /*) F3="$FIRST_3"    ;; *) F3="/videos/$FIRST_3"    ;; esac
case "$MUSIC_RAW" in /*) MUSIC="$MUSIC_RAW";; *) MUSIC="/videos/$MUSIC_RAW";; esac

# Sanity checks
[ -f "$IN" ]    || { echo "ERROR: Input video not found: $IN" >&2; exit 1; }
[ -f "$F3" ]    || { echo "ERROR: Overlay not found: $F3" >&2; exit 1; }
[ -f "$MUSIC" ] || { echo "ERROR: Music not found: $MUSIC" >&2; exit 1; }

rm -f -- "$OUT" || true

# Explanation:
# - Overlay fades out at 2.5s (0.5s crossfade).
# - Voice track from IN is boosted 1.5x louder.
# - Music stays at 0.1 volume.
# - Overlay scales up and centers.

ffmpeg -hide_banner -loglevel error -y -nostdin -threads 2 \
  -i "$IN" \
  -i "$F3" \
  -i "$MUSIC" \
  -filter_complex "
    [1:v]trim=0:3,setpts=PTS-STARTPTS,scale=iw*1:-1,fade=t=out:st=2.5:d=0.5:alpha=1[overlayfit]; \
    [0:v]setpts=PTS-STARTPTS[base]; \
    [base][overlayfit]overlay=(W-w)/2:(H-h)/2:enable='between(t,0,3)'[vout]; \
    [0:a]volume=1.5[a0]; \
    [2:a]volume=0.1[a2]; \
    [a0][a2]amix=inputs=2:duration=first:dropout_transition=3[aout]
  " \
  -map "[vout]" -map "[aout]" \
  -c:v libx264 -preset veryfast -crf 18 -pix_fmt yuv420p \
  -c:a aac -b:a 192k \
  -movflags +faststart \
  "$OUT"

printf '{"output":"%s"}' "$OUT"

=#!/bin/sh
set -eu

RID="{{ $('Update Voice').item.json.id }}"

INPUT="/videos/${RID}_xvideo.mp4"
VOICE="/videos/${RID}_voice_full.mp3"
OUTPUT="/videos/${RID}_xvideo_9x16.mp4"

ffmpeg -y \
  -i "$INPUT" \
  -i "$VOICE" \
  -filter_complex "\
    color=c=black:s=1080x1920:d=999[bg]; \
    [0:v]scale=1080:-1[vid]; \
    [bg][vid]overlay=(W-w)/2:(H-h)/2:eval=init,format=yuv420p[v]" \
  -map "[v]" -map 1:a \
  -c:v libx264 -c:a aac -pix_fmt yuv420p \
  -shortest -movflags +faststart "$OUTPUT"

printf '{\"output\":\"%s\"}' \"$OUTPUT\"

=#!/bin/sh
set -eu

IN="/videos/{{ $('Update Voice').item.json.id }}_video_only.mp4"
SUBS="/videos/{{ $('Update Voice').item.json.id }}_subtitles.ass"
OUT="/videos/{{ $('Update Voice').item.json.id }}_output.mp4"

ffmpeg -y -i "$IN" -vf "subtitles='$SUBS'" -c:v libx264 -c:a copy "$OUT"

printf '{"output":"%s"}' "$OUT"

values.content:
=You are a viral YouTube Shorts scriptwriter.
	•	Read the target length (seconds) and compute WORD_BUDGET = floor(seconds * WPS). Default WPS = {{ $json.wps || 2.4 }} .
	•	Output one script only, no pre/post text.
	•	Never exceed WORD_BUDGET words. If your draft is longer, compress before replying.
	•	6th-grade reading level.
	•	First sentence = announcement of exactly what happened.
	•	2nd sentence starts the details; include specific facts from the inspiration.
	•	the text should be written in the style interesting conversational tone
	•	Keep in mind "whats in it for you" make it benefit driven
	•	End with: a one sentence close of a shocking statement!



Do not write more words than 2.4 words per second
Do not write less words than 2.4 words per second

=find out more information about: 
{{ $json.tweets[0].text }}

Posted by {{ $json.tweets[0].author.userName }} on x. 

They may have annoucned something or an update?
I want you to find as many facts and information about it.

Come back with at least 500 words

values.content:
=You are a caption generator that returns ONLY a single valid JSON object with these keys:
“Instagram caption”, “Facebook caption”, “Twitter caption”, “TikTok caption”, “YouTube short caption”, “YouTube short title”, “LinkedIn caption”, “Bluesky caption”, “Threads caption”, “Pinterest caption”.

Goal
Create concise, platform-tailored captions for a short-form video. The user will provide a YouTube or short-form script as input. Extract the hook, the core benefit, and 1 to 2 concrete specifics. Focus entirely on delivering value, clarity, and insight — do NOT include engagement prompts or calls to action like “subscribe,” “follow,” “comment,” “share,” or “watch till the end.” Never Include Hashtags. 

Inputs you will receive
	•	video_script: the full script or outline
	•	topic or product: optional
	•	target_audience: optional
	•	hashtags_or_keywords: optional
	•	tone: optional (default energetic, helpful)

Global rules
	•	Output JSON only. No extra text, no markdown, no comments.
	•	Do not include newline characters inside any value unless the platform rules below explicitly allow line breaks.
	•	Never fabricate facts. Derive claims from the video_script.
	•	Keep captions distinct across platforms. No copy-paste.
	•	Prefer 1 emoji max where appropriate. If tone is professional, use zero.
	•	Avoid platform-specific handles unless provided.
	•	Language: match the language of the video_script.
	•	No engagement CTAs in any caption (no subscribe, follow, like, comment, save, share, etc.). Focus on delivering insight or value.

Platform styles and constraints
	1.	Instagram caption

	•	Aim 80 to 120 characters.
	•	One short hook plus clear benefit.
	•	Optional single emoji near the start.
	•	No links.
	•	No CTA.

	2.	Facebook caption

	•	Aim 100 to 160 characters.
	•	Friendly, helpful, and clear. One value-focused sentence.
	•	No CTA.

	3.	Twitter caption

	•	Aim 80 to 120 characters.
	•	Tight hook + 1 key detail.
	•	Avoid hashtags. Avoid emojis unless informal.
	•	No CTA.

	4.	TikTok caption

	•	Aim 60 to 100 characters.
	•	High-energy hook + value clarity.
	•	No CTA.

	5.	YouTube short caption

	•	Aim 100 to 150 characters.
	•	Clearly state the value viewers get in one line.
	•	No CTA.

	6.	YouTube short title

	•	Aim 40 to 60 characters.
	•	Lead with outcome or transformation with a strong verb.
	•	No clickbait brackets.
	•	No CTA.

	7.	LinkedIn caption

	•	Aim 140 to 200 characters.
	•	Professional, benefit-led, with one specific insight from the script.
	•	No emojis unless casual industry.
	•	No hashtags.
	•	No CTA.

	8.	Bluesky caption

	•	Aim 80 to 120 characters.
	•	Conversational, slightly tech-savvy tone.
	•	No hashtags. No emojis unless casual.
	•	No CTA.

	9.	Threads caption

	•	Aim 80 to 120 characters.
	•	Casual, community-driven tone.
	•	1 emoji max if natural.
	•	No hashtags.
	•	No CTA.

	10.	Pinterest caption

	•	Aim 100 to 150 characters.
	•	Inspire curiosity or promise a benefit.
	•	1 to 2 hashtags allowed.
	•	No emojis unless lifestyle tone.
	•	No CTA.

Formatting rules
	•	Escape any internal quotes to ensure valid JSON.
	•	Do not exceed platform character targets.
	•	If an input is missing, make a safe reasonable assumption. Do not leave fields empty.

Return format:
{
“Instagram caption”: “…”,
“Facebook caption”: “…”,
“Twitter caption”: “…”,
“TikTok caption”: “…”,
“YouTube short caption”: “…”,
“YouTube short title”: “…”,
“LinkedIn caption”: “…”,
“Bluesky caption”: “…”,
“Threads caption”: “…”,
“Pinterest caption”: “…”
}

parameters.command:
=# Dynamic values
TEXT="{{ $('Generate Hook').item.json.message.content }}"
AUTHOR="@{{ $('TwitterAPI.io1').item.json.tweets[0].author.userName }}"

# Find best available bold font on the system
FONT=$(fc-match -f '%{file}' ':weight=200:style=Bold' 2>/dev/null || echo "")

# Build fontfile arg
FONTARG=""
if [ -n "$FONT" ] && [ -f "$FONT" ]; then
  FONTARG="fontfile=${FONT}:"
fi

ffmpeg -y \
  -i "/videos/{{ $('Update Script1').item.json.id }}_xvideo.mp4" \
  -filter_complex "
    color=c=black:s=1080x1920:d=999[bg];
    [0:v]scale=1080:-1[vid];
    [bg][vid]overlay=(W-w)/2:(H-h)/2,
    drawtext=${FONTARG}text='${TEXT}':fontsize=64:fontcolor=black@0.4:x=(w-text_w)/2+3:y=153:enable='lt(t\,3)',
    drawtext=${FONTARG}text='${TEXT}':fontsize=64:fontcolor=white:borderw=3:bordercolor=black:x=(w-text_w)/2:y=150:enable='lt(t\,3)',
    drawtext=${FONTARG}text='${AUTHOR}':fontsize=28:fontcolor=white:shadowx=2:shadowy=2:shadowcolor=black@0.6:x=30:y=h-60:enable='lt(t\,3)'[v]
  " \
  -map "[v]" -map 0:a? \
  -c:v libx264 -pix_fmt yuv420p -crf 18 -preset veryfast \
  -c:a aac -movflags +faststart -shortest \
  /videos/{{ $('Update Script1').item.json.id }}_output.mp4

values.content:
=You are a video hook generator. Given a YouTube Shorts script, generate ONE catchy hook (3-5 words max) for a text overlay. Rules:
- ALL CAPS
- Maximum 5 words
- Attention-grabbing, relates to the main topic
- Always adding including the AI tool name
- Can end with ! or ?
- Output ONLY the hook text, nothing else

=# knobs

color=c=black:size=1080x1920:d=360[bg];
[0:v]scale=1080:${TOP_H}:flags=bicubic[top];
[1:v]scale=1080:-2[vs];
[vs]crop=w=1080:h=${BOTTOM_H}:x=0:y=${CROP_TOP}[bot];
[bg][top]overlay=x=0:y=0[tmp];
[tmp][bot]overlay=x=0:y=${BOT_Y}[v];
[1:a]volume=1.5[a]

=#!/bin/sh
set -eu -o pipefail

RID="{{ $('Update Voice').item.json.id }}"
IN_RAW="${RID}_xvideo_9x16.mp4"
FIRST_3="${RID}_output_vertical.mp4"
MUSIC_RAW="${RID}_music.mp3"

OUT="/videos/${RID}_video_only.mp4"

# Resolve absolute paths
case "$IN_RAW"    in /*) IN="$IN_RAW"     ;; *) IN="/videos/$IN_RAW"     ;; esac
case "$FIRST_3"   in /*) F3="$FIRST_3"    ;; *) F3="/videos/$FIRST_3"    ;; esac
case "$MUSIC_RAW" in /*) MUSIC="$MUSIC_RAW";; *) MUSIC="/videos/$MUSIC_RAW";; esac

# Sanity checks
[ -f "$IN" ]    || { echo "ERROR: Input video not found: $IN" >&2; exit 1; }
[ -f "$F3" ]    || { echo "ERROR: Overlay not found: $F3" >&2; exit 1; }
[ -f "$MUSIC" ] || { echo "ERROR: Music not found: $MUSIC" >&2; exit 1; }

rm -f -- "$OUT" || true

# Explanation:
# - Overlay fades out at 2.5s (0.5s crossfade).
# - Voice track from IN is boosted 1.5x louder.
# - Music stays at 0.1 volume.
# - Overlay scales up and centers.

ffmpeg -hide_banner -loglevel error -y -nostdin -threads 2 \
  -i "$IN" \
  -i "$F3" \
  -i "$MUSIC" \
  -filter_complex "
    [1:v]trim=0:3,setpts=PTS-STARTPTS,scale=iw*1:-1,fade=t=out:st=2.5:d=0.5:alpha=1[overlayfit]; \
    [0:v]setpts=PTS-STARTPTS[base]; \
    [base][overlayfit]overlay=(W-w)/2:(H-h)/2:enable='between(t,0,3)'[vout]; \
    [0:a]volume=1.5[a0]; \
    [2:a]volume=0.1[a2]; \
    [a0][a2]amix=inputs=2:duration=first:dropout_transition=3[aout]
  " \
  -map "[vout]" -map "[aout]" \
  -c:v libx264 -preset veryfast -crf 18 -pix_fmt yuv420p \
  -c:a aac -b:a 192k \
  -movflags +faststart \
  "$OUT"

printf '{"output":"%s"}' "$OUT"

=#!/bin/sh
set -eu

RID="{{ $('Update Voice').item.json.id }}"

INPUT="/videos/${RID}_xvideo.mp4"
VOICE="/videos/${RID}_voice_full.mp3"
OUTPUT="/videos/${RID}_xvideo_9x16.mp4"

ffmpeg -y \
  -i "$INPUT" \
  -i "$VOICE" \
  -filter_complex "\
    color=c=black:s=1080x1920:d=999[bg]; \
    [0:v]scale=1080:-1[vid]; \
    [bg][vid]overlay=(W-w)/2:(H-h)/2:eval=init,format=yuv420p[v]" \
  -map "[v]" -map 1:a \
  -c:v libx264 -c:a aac -pix_fmt yuv420p \
  -shortest -movflags +faststart "$OUTPUT"

printf '{\"output\":\"%s\"}' \"$OUTPUT\"

=#!/bin/sh
set -eu

IN="/videos/{{ $('Update Voice').item.json.id }}_video_only.mp4"
SUBS="/videos/{{ $('Update Voice').item.json.id }}_subtitles.ass"
OUT="/videos/{{ $('Update Voice').item.json.id }}_output.mp4"

ffmpeg -y -i "$IN" -vf "subtitles='$SUBS'" -c:v libx264 -c:a copy "$OUT"

printf '{"output":"%s"}' "$OUT"

“TikTok caption”: “…”,
“YouTube short caption”: “…”,
“YouTube short title”: “…”,
“LinkedIn caption”: “…”,
“Bluesky caption”: “…”,
“Threads caption”: “…”,
“Pinterest caption”: “…”
}

parameters.command:
=# Dynamic values
TEXT="{{ $('Generate Hook').item.json.message.content }}"
AUTHOR="@{{ $('TwitterAPI.io1').item.json.tweets[0].author.userName }}"

# Find best available bold font on the system
FONT=$(fc-match -f '%{file}' ':weight=200:style=Bold' 2>/dev/null || echo "")

# Build fontfile arg
FONTARG=""
if [ -n "$FONT" ] && [ -f "$FONT" ]; then
  FONTARG="fontfile=${FONT}:"
fi

ffmpeg -y \
  -i "/videos/{{ $('Update Script1').item.json.id }}_xvideo.mp4" \
  -filter_complex "
    color=c=black:s=1080x1920:d=999[bg];
    [0:v]scale=1080:-1[vid];
    [bg][vid]overlay=(W-w)/2:(H-h)/2,
    drawtext=${FONTARG}text='${TEXT}':fontsize=64:fontcolor=black@0.4:x=(w-text_w)/2+3:y=153:enable='lt(t\,3)',
    drawtext=${FONTARG}text='${TEXT}':fontsize=64:fontcolor=white:borderw=3:bordercolor=black:x=(w-text_w)/2:y=150:enable='lt(t\,3)',
    drawtext=${FONTARG}text='${AUTHOR}':fontsize=28:fontcolor=white:shadowx=2:shadowy=2:shadowcolor=black@0.6:x=30:y=h-60:enable='lt(t\,3)'[v]
  " \
  -map "[v]" -map 0:a? \
  -c:v libx264 -pix_fmt yuv420p -crf 18 -preset veryfast \
  -c:a aac -movflags +faststart -shortest \
  /videos/{{ $('Update Script1').item.json.id }}_output.mp4

values.content:
=You are a video hook generator. Given a YouTube Shorts script, generate ONE catchy hook (3-5 words max) for a text overlay. Rules:
- ALL CAPS
- Maximum 5 words
- Attention-grabbing, relates to the main topic
- Always adding including the AI tool name
- Can end with ! or ?
- Output ONLY the hook text, nothing else

=You are a caption generator that returns ONLY a single valid JSON object with these keys:
“Instagram caption”, “Facebook caption”, “Twitter caption”, “TikTok caption”, “YouTube short caption”, “YouTube short title”, “LinkedIn caption”, “Bluesky caption”, “Threads caption”, “Pinterest caption”.

Goal
Create concise, platform-tailored captions for a short-form video. The user will provide a YouTube or short-form script as input. Extract the hook, the core benefit, and 1 to 2 concrete specifics. Focus entirely on delivering value, clarity, and insight — do NOT include engagement prompts or calls to action like “subscribe,” “follow,” “comment,” “share,” or “watch till the end.” Never Include Hashtags. 

Inputs you will receive
	•	video_script: the full script or outline
	•	topic or product: optional
	•	target_audience: optional
	•	hashtags_or_keywords: optional
	•	tone: optional (default energetic, helpful)

Global rules
	•	Output JSON only. No extra text, no markdown, no comments.
	•	Do not include newline characters inside any value unless the platform rules below explicitly allow line breaks.
	•	Never fabricate facts. Derive claims from the video_script.
	•	Keep captions distinct across platforms. No copy-paste.
	•	Prefer 1 emoji max where appropriate. If tone is professional, use zero.
	•	Avoid platform-specific handles unless provided.
	•	Language: match the language of the video_script.
	•	No engagement CTAs in any caption (no subscribe, follow, like, comment, save, share, etc.). Focus on delivering insight or value.

Platform styles and constraints
	1.	Instagram caption

	•	Aim 80 to 120 characters.
	•	One short hook plus clear benefit.
	•	Optional single emoji near the start.
	•	No links.
	•	No CTA.

	2.	Facebook caption

	•	Aim 100 to 160 characters.
	•	Friendly, helpful, and clear. One value-focused sentence.
	•	No CTA.

	3.	Twitter caption

	•	Aim 80 to 120 characters.
	•	Tight hook + 1 key detail.
	•	Avoid hashtags. Avoid emojis unless informal.
	•	No CTA.

	4.	TikTok caption

	•	Aim 60 to 100 characters.
	•	High-energy hook + value clarity.
	•	No CTA.

	5.	YouTube short caption

	•	Aim 100 to 150 characters.
	•	Clearly state the value viewers get in one line.
	•	No CTA.

	6.	YouTube short title

	•	Aim 40 to 60 characters.
	•	Lead with outcome or transformation with a strong verb.
	•	No clickbait brackets.
	•	No CTA.

	7.	LinkedIn caption

	•	Aim 140 to 200 characters.
	•	Professional, benefit-led, with one specific insight from the script.
	•	No emojis unless casual industry.
	•	No hashtags.
	•	No CTA.

	8.	Bluesky caption

	•	Aim 80 to 120 characters.
	•	Conversational, slightly tech-savvy tone.
	•	No hashtags. No emojis unless casual.
	•	No CTA.

	9.	Threads caption

	•	Aim 80 to 120 characters.
	•	Casual, community-driven tone.
	•	1 emoji max if natural.
	•	No hashtags.
	•	No CTA.

	10.	Pinterest caption

	•	Aim 100 to 150 characters.
	•	Inspire curiosity or promise a benefit.
	•	1 to 2 hashtags allowed.
	•	No emojis unless lifestyle tone.
	•	No CTA.

Formatting rules
	•	Escape any internal quotes to ensure valid JSON.
	•	Do not exceed platform character targets.
	•	If an input is missing, make a safe reasonable assumption. Do not leave fields empty.

Return format:
{
“Instagram caption”: “…”,
“Facebook caption”: “…”,
“Twitter caption”: “…”,
“TikTok caption”: “…”,
“YouTube short caption”: “…”,
“YouTube short title”: “…”,
“LinkedIn caption”: “…”,
“Bluesky caption”: “…”,
“Threads caption”: “…”,
“Pinterest caption”: “…”
}
